title: BiomekFXp Robot Minipreps (RoboPreps)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fn4kp","text":"RoboPreps are great for high-throughput cloning and subcloning, especially if you’re going to be doing Maxipreps afterward anyway. RoboPreps must be done in 96 well format as of now, though future generations may one day break through this technological barrier.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Pick colonies into 1 mL, LB cultures into a 96-well assay block and grow at 37 °C overnight with shaking.
Pellet the cells by spinning at for 5-15 minutes 00:05:00 .
Remove the media by quickly inverting the block over a waste container. If intended for later use, you can freeze the block in -20 °C for several weeks or keep it at 4 °C for hours.
For a batch lysis, load the pelleted cultures onto the BiomekFXp robot using the qialyse2 program.

For the qialyse2 program, you need to assemble a two-component filter consisting of a low-profille 1.2 ml 96-well block and a 96-well filter block . They should be affixed to each other using tape as shown before loading on to the robot deck. For simplicity, use inverted tip-box covers as reservoirs for the resuspension, lysis, and neutralization buffers. Qiagen Buffers P1, P2, and P3 can be used as the resuspension, lysis, and neutralization buffers (to be put in the reservoirs on the robot deck), but similar home-made buffers should be fine.
Resuspend cell pellet in 80 µL, P1 buffer and shake.

Spin down the lysate through the filter block at for at least 00:05:00 (longer is fine). Make sure to balance with a blank filter. Another blank with improper dimensions may cause an imbalance error, even if the weight matches the filter.
Collect the block and remove the filter. Take care not to jostle the low-profile 96-well block, since the flow-through can easily splash between wells.
Transfer the flow-through to an Axygen 96-well block and load it on to the robot using the RoboPrep program.
Mix filtered cell lysate with 165 µL, SPRI-Bead binding buffer
Add 80 µL, P2 and shake.
Pause for 00:02:00 .
Add 80 µL, P3 buffer and shake for 00:01:40 (100 seconds).
Transfer contents to assembled filter apparatus.
Wash with 70% EtOH. (2/3)
Wash with 70% EtOH. (1/3)
Wash with 70% EtOH supplemented with 1% TritonX-114.
Remove Supernatant.
Place on magnet and allow separation.
Incubate Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Wash with 70% EtOH. (3/3)
Remove all ethanol and dry beads for 00:05:00 .
Resuspend beads in 50 µL, elution buffer .
Remove from beads.
creator: James Angstman
